title: Frequently Asked Questions
subtitle: Everything you need to know about salary expectations and navigating the {name} tool.
name: Am I Underpaid?

underpaid.question: Am I being underpaid?
underpaid.answer: The best way to determine if you are undervalued is to calculate your MCA Score. By benchmarking your pay against live market demand and federal BLS data, we provide an objective look at your current market value.

averageWage.question: What is the typical compensation for my role?
averageWage.answer: Average compensation depends heavily on your location, industry, and experience level. By running a search, you can view the median salary and distribution curve for your exact position.

expect.question: What salary should I expect?
expect.answer: You should expect a salary that aligns with current market rates. Whether you are negotiating a raise or applying for a new job, knowing the expected salary range gives you the power to negotiate fairly.

fairPay.question: What is a 'Competitive' MCA Score?
fairPay.answer: An MCA Score between 40 and 79 is generally considered 'Competitive' or 'Strong Alignment.' If your score is above 80, you are a Market Leader. If it falls below 40, it may be time to negotiate a raise or explore new opportunities.

isMySalaryRight.question: Is my salary right for my role?
isMySalaryRight.answer: A 'right' salary should reflect the current demand for your skills. Since market conditions change, using a real-time calculator helps you verify if your current pay matches the most recent job listings and government benchmarks.

whyDoINeedToKnow.question: Why do I need to know my market rate?
whyDoINeedToKnow.answer: Knowing your market rate gives you leverage during salary negotiations and performance reviews. It ensures you aren't leaving money on the table and helps you make informed decisions about your career progression.

jobTitle.question: How do I enter my job title?
jobTitle.answer: Start typing your job title and our smart autocomplete will suggest government standard industry roles. This helps ensure your salary comparison is based on accurate and relevant data.

cantFindJobTitle.question: What if I can't find my exact job title?
cantFindJobTitle.answer: Our suggestions are based on federal Standard Occupational Classifications (SOC). Don't worry if your internal company title isn't there—just enter it anyway, and our algorithm will map you to the closest federal equivalent.

salaryScare.question: What if I don't want to enter my salary?
salaryScare.answer: We understand the hesitation around sharing salary information. While entering your current salary helps us provide a direct comparison specific to you, you can choose to leave it blank. Our tool will still give you valuable insights based on the average salary for your role and location.

gdpr.question: What do you do with my data?
gdpr.answer: We take your privacy seriously. Any data you enter is used solely to provide you with accurate salary comparisons and insights. We do not sell or share your personal information with third parties. For more details, please refer to our Privacy Policy.

whatIfIAmFairlyPaid.question: What if the tool says I am fairly paid?
whatIfIAmFairlyPaid.answer: That is great news! Being fairly paid means your employer is keeping up with market trends. You can use this data during your next review to confirm your value and discuss future growth or additional benefits.

whatIfIAmWellPaid.question: What if I am already well paid?
whatIfIAmWellPaid.answer: If you are above the market average, you are in a strong position. Use our insights to see how much higher the top 10% of earners in your field are making and what skills might be required to stay at that level.

whatIsMca.question: What is an MCA Score?
whatIsMca.answer: MCA stands for Market Compensation Alignment. It is a proprietary score from 1 to 99 that measures how your base pay aligns with the current US market. A higher score indicates you are a Market Leader, while a lower score suggest your compensation is below the current market rate.

howIsItCalculated.question: How is my MCA Score calculated?
howIsItCalculated.answer: Our engine uses a 'Triangulated' logic. We cross-reference official federal BLS statistics, national economic trends, and over 7,000,000+ live US job listings to identify exactly where your salary sits in today's economy.

locationImpact.question: Does my location affect my MCA Score?
locationImpact.answer: Absolutely. Our engine applies dynamic regional modifiers. A $100k salary in San Francisco will result in a different MCA Score than the same salary in Austin, reflecting the specific cost of labor in your state or city.

marketValue.question: How is market value determined?
marketValue.answer: Market value is a reflection of real-time supply and demand. Our platform determines this by aggregating over 7,000,000 active job postings alongside federal BLS benchmarks to establish the current 'going rate'.

dataPillars.question: What data defines the salary range?
dataPillars.answer: We calculate ranges using three primary pillars: live job listings (active market demand), official occupational benchmarks, and regional cost-of-labor adjustments. This provides a comprehensive view of the median and distribution curve.

competitiveOffer.question: What is considered a 'competitive' salary offer?
competitiveOffer.answer: A competitive offer typically aligns with the 50th percentile (median) or higher of active listings. For employers, this ensures high applicant volume; for candidates, it ensures fair compensation relative to the current market.

benchmarkingFrequency.question: How often should a role be benchmarked?
benchmarkingFrequency.answer: The market is dynamic. Because we analyze live listings, our data reflects economic shifts in real-time. We recommend benchmarking roles quarterly to ensure pay ranges remain aligned with the latest hiring trends.

employerUtility.question: Can I use this tool to set company compensation bands?
employerUtility.answer: Absolutely. Benchmark My Role is designed to help HR teams and hiring managers validate their pay ranges. By checking your intended budget against live data, you ensure your open position is competitive enough to attract top US talent.

strategicAdvantage.question: Why is objective benchmarking essential?
strategicAdvantage.answer: For employers, it prevents talent attrition. For professionals, it provides objective evidence for data-backed negotiations. Transparency reduces friction on both sides of the hiring table, leading to faster, fairer placements.

roleSelection.question: How do I select the correct role?
roleSelection.answer: Start typing the job function. Our system uses standardized occupational classifications to ensure the data you see is categorized correctly against industry peers, rather than just matching an internal company title.

uniqueTitles.question: What if I have a unique or hybrid job title?
uniqueTitles.answer: If an exact match isn't available, select the role that represents the core 70% of the responsibilities. Our algorithm cross-references your input against the closest market equivalents to provide a reliable benchmark.

privacy.question: Is my research anonymous?
privacy.answer: Yes. Benchmark My Role is a transparency-first platform. You can research market rates without entering personal salary details. Any data provided is anonymized to improve the accuracy of our real-time models.

gdpr.question: How do you handle data privacy?
gdpr.answer: We adhere to strict data protection standards. We do not sell personal data to third parties. Our purpose is to provide aggregate market insights, and all individual inputs are treated with professional-grade security.

percentileData.question: How should I interpret the 'Market Leader' (90th percentile) data?
percentileData.answer: For employers, this identifies what is required to headhunt elite talent. For applicants, the 90th percentile represents the rate usually reserved for highly specialized skills or leadership roles in high-competition regions.

recruitmentEdge.question: How does the MCA Score help with talent acquisition?
recruitmentEdge.answer: The MCA Score provides an objective third-party metric to justify your compensation bands. Using a data-driven score helps recruiters close candidates faster by proving that an offer is 'Market Leading' based on 7,000,000+ live data points.
